body.title: uu <verb> must degrade to the source tree when the venv is gone — it's a rescue net, not a venv-dependent view
body.intention: I intend that `uu <verb>` comes up on a degradation ladder — activated venv, else venv python, else the raw source tree via PYTHONPATH, else a soft 'here is what is missing' message — so the zero-inference view layer starts whenever ANY usable piece is present and never dies with a raw traceback, honoring CP6 (it is a rescue net).
body.description:
BUG (CP6 rescue-net floor). `uu opentickets` from a plain terminal tracebacks with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'unseen_university'` (uu_tickets.py:21). `bin/uu` execs the BARE `python3`; the editable install lives only in `.venv/...site-packages`, so a shell without the venv activated gets system python and every verb dies at import.

CORRECTED INTENT (per Akien 2026-07-11): "runs in a bare shell" is a FLOOR, not a demand to avoid the venv. `uu` is the rescue net — the thing you reach for exactly when the environment (or CC) is broken. So it must come up on a DEGRADATION LADDER, bringing up as much as the pieces allow:
  1. venv present + activated -> use it as-is.
  2. venv present, not activated -> resolve & exec `$UU_ROOT/.venv/bin/python3`.
  3. venv gone/broken but SOURCE TREE present -> import straight from `$UU_ROOT` (system python3 + `PYTHONPATH=$UU_ROOT`),      NO install, NO venv. PROVEN VIABLE: `env -i PYTHONPATH=$UU_ROOT /usr/bin/python3 -c 'from unseen_university import      ticket_store, ticket_status'` imports clean and pulls ZERO heavy deps (no psycopg2/loguru/pydantic).
  4. even that broken -> FAIL SOFT: print what is missing + the next step; never a raw traceback.

My first fix ('find venv, else system python3') only covers rungs 1-2 and still dies at rung 3 — the exact scenario that defines a rescue net. Reconciles with [[reference_editable_finder_beats_pythonpath]]: the editable venv install stays the PRIMARY path; PYTHONPATH-to-source is the RESCUE rung below it, not a replacement.

Proposed shape in `bin/uu`: resolve an interpreter+env once at the top — `PY="$UU_ROOT/.venv/bin/python3"`; if executable run `exec "$PY" ...`; else `exec env PYTHONPATH="$UU_ROOT" python3 ...`; if no python3 at all, print a soft diagnostic and exit nonzero. One resolver, homogeneous across all current+future verbs ([[feedback_homogeneity_over_special_case]]).

**Affected files:** bin/uu (interpreter+env resolver; the `exec python3` lines for mytickets/opentickets/recall/research/device). No change to the handler scripts. tests/ — new rescue-ladder test.
**Design rules:** ticket_design_checks (test-plan-or-why-not). no-sqlite N/A. homogeneity-over-special-case (one resolver). CP6 fail-soft.
**Scope boundary:** IN — bin/uu degradation ladder (venv -> venv-python -> source-tree PYTHONPATH -> soft fail) + a test that proves rung 3 (no venv) still serves the view. OUT — the `.bashrc -> uu_bash_profile.sh` env migration (T-bashrc-uu-migration-finish); auto-CREATING/repairing the venv (rescueclaude owns venv bootstrap); the same ladder for superclaude/rescueclaude (their own tickets — note below); changing what verbs display.
**Test plan:** proof-on-close. A test runs `bin/uu opentickets` in a subprocess with a scrubbed env AND the venv made unreachable (e.g. UU_ROOT pointed at a copy with no .venv, or venv dir hidden), asserting exit 0 and real ticket output with NO `ModuleNotFoundError` — i.e. it served off the source tree. RED on today's `exec python3` (import fails at rung 3), GREEN once uu falls through to `PYTHONPATH=$UU_ROOT`.
**Proof obligation:** a hollow build cannot pass the no-venv subprocess test — the fix must serve the view from the raw source tree with the venv absent. This discharges the sub-intention that the rescue net start whenever the source is present, not only when the venv is healthy.
**Completion criteria:** `uu opentickets` works from (a) a non-activated shell with venv and (b) a shell with NO venv at all (source-tree rung); a missing python3 yields a soft diagnostic, not a traceback; the no-venv test passes (red-before/green-after).
body.result: Fixed: bin/uu resolves an interpreter down a rescue ladder (venv python by abs path -> source tree via PYTHONPATH=$UU_ROOT -> fail soft). uu opentickets now runs in a bare shell (shows 400 open tickets); old exec python3 tracebacked with ModuleNotFoundError. Proof: tests/test_uu_dispatcher.py::test_bare_shell_uses_venv_python_when_not_on_path (red=AssertionError->green), + rung-3 no-venv source-tree test. proof_emitter cert @bc1fef2f.
